source: Numerics/Data/Interpolation/Bilinear.cs; adapted from the new v2.1.4 Test_Bilinear.cs Test_LogarithmicFloorMatchesLinearInterpolation, which added the first upstream regression coverage proving Bilinear's guarded log10 floor matches Linear's.
reference: Numerics @ 2a0357a. Args convention (this port's own -- see fixtures/README.md and bilinear_log_floor_value() in core/tests/test_fixtures.cpp): args = [x1_query, x2_query] against a FIXED 3x3 identity grid ({0, 1E-15, 1} on both axes, y[i][j] = x1_values[i] for every j) with X1Transform/X2Transform/YTransform all Logarithmic -- the exact grid the upstream test uses. v2.1.4 fixed Bilinear's Logarithmic transform to call Tools.Log10 (guarded: values < 1E-16 clamp to 1E-16 rather than producing -Infinity) instead of plain Math.Log10, matching what Linear.cs already did (see bilinear.hpp's file header, docs/upstream-csharp-issues.md marked RESOLVED). Before the fix, both cases below returned NaN: `log10(0)` is -Infinity, and the interpolation formula's `(x - x1i)` numerator/denominator both become `-Infinity - -Infinity`, i.e. NaN. Every value was generated by running the case's inputs through tools/oracle_emitter --dump against the REAL C# Bilinear class and confirming the printed actuals reproduce via verify_oracles.py.

assertions.source: curated via oracle_emitter --dump; adapted from Test_LogarithmicFloorMatchesLinearInterpolation's `atZero` assertion (there only checked for finiteness; this port additionally pins the exact guarded-floor value, 10^clamped_log10(0) = 10^(-16))

curated via oracle_emitter --dump; adapted from Test_LogarithmicFloorMatchesLinearInterpolation's `belowFloor` assertion, which compares this value against a companion Linear interpolation over the same identity grid to 1E-28 -- both reduce to the same 10^clamped_log10(5E-17) = 10^(-16) expression, so pinning this one value against the real C# Bilinear class covers the equivalence
